trigger.event: Q4 2022 earnings reveal CHF 110.5 billion deposit outflows
trigger.description: On February 9, 2023, Credit Suisse reported Q4 2022 earnings revealing CHF 110.5 billion in net new asset outflows during the quarter.

trigger.event: Q4 2022 earnings reveal CHF 110.5 billion deposit outflows

active_hypotheses.name: Restructuring Plan Failed to Restore Confidence
active_hypotheses.description: October 2022 restructuring announcement triggered further outflows

eliminated_hypotheses.name: Undisclosed AT1 Write-down Risk
eliminated_hypotheses.reason: Structural evidence shows AT1 terms were fully disclosed in public filings since 2019

eliminated_hypotheses.name: Hidden Regulatory Capital Breach
eliminated_hypotheses.reason: Q4 earnings showed CET1 ratio at 14.1%, well above regulatory minimums

eliminated_hypotheses.name: Liquidity Crisis (Imminent Insolvency)
eliminated_hypotheses.reason: LCR maintained above 144% throughout Q4, no actual liquidity stress

eliminated_hypotheses.name: Undisclosed Counterparty Exposure
eliminated_hypotheses.reason: Counterparty disclosures in annual report showed no concentrated risk

alert.summary: 3 risk factors confirmed after eliminating 4 hypotheses

case_file.compressed_reasoning: Investigation converged after 3 cycles. Started with 7 hypotheses about CHF 110.5B deposit outflows. Eliminated 4 hypotheses through evidence contradictions: (1) AT1 write-down risk was disclosed, not hidden (2) Capital ratios adequate (3) No actual liquidity crisis (4) No undisclosed counterparty risk. Final diagnosis: Reputational crisis from 2021 scandals, failed restructuring plan, and systematic wealth management client exodus. Root cause is confidence collapse, not solvency.
